meaning: v.呼气,发出,发散,<古>使蒸发
examples: As the lungs exhale this waste, gas is expelled into the atmosphere.
Generally, you exhale on the effort, if doing weights, karate or boxing, and inhale on the return.
Without gravity to help circulate air, the carbon dioxide you exhale has a tendency to form an invisible cloud around your head.
Rather than take one deep sniff, take two or three short inhalations and then exhale.
Count slowly to two on the inhale and do the same on the exhale.
